ops.title: FINAL 2026-07 campaign verdict: ccx COSTS tokens on sonnet+opus (schema tax on H, round-trips on T) at BETTER accuracy; flood ceiling in real sessions only 3.6-3.9% of T; redesign staged in bench/analysis/; three accounting bugs fixed (msgid billing, bille

ops.body:
Final state of the ccx token benchmark, 2026-07-12 (supersedes the interim framing; durable narrative lives in bench/FINDINGS.md — read that first, this notes what's NOT written there).

**Verdict (both campaigns, all gates green, ~$200 total):** FAIL all 4 model×arm cells. ccx-mcp H −13.9%/−16.7% (pure MCP schema tax, ~5.3k/task, 9 of 18 tools never called); ccx-cli T −22.4%/−16.5% (round-trips: +0.7 billed calls × ~47k cache re-read = 92% of the loss; `ccx exec` used 0/351 runs). Tool-result tokens ~3-4× baseline (the microbench's 90% compares against a full-file Read the baseline never performs; its real comparator is a 5-line grep window). Accuracy FAVORS ccx (e.g. opus cli 96.1% vs 93.8) — part of the token cost bought correctness (verified: structural diff wins diff-tornado-response 5/5 vs 1/5).

**The reframe that matters:** the flood thesis was measured for the first time (bench/analysis/flood-audit-report.md): real-session guard-rewrite ceiling is only 3.6–3.9% of billed T (median 0.18%, p90 6.2%, max 28.9%); "Read=71.6%" is dead (now 41.7% of tool tokens); the MCP schema tax compounds to ~2.4–3.9% at real session lengths and CANCELS the ceiling — the CLI lane is the only positive-headroom path. Haiku floods 2.4× more (weak-model axis is the honest deployment claim). Redesign proposal (bench/analysis/bench-redesign-proposal.md): staged A(free audit, done)→B(flood-inducing tasks)→C(session-capacity/compaction headline)+D(obfuscated-fork corpus); session capacity, not marginal token %, is the legible claim. User verdict on the old design: it "does not reflect the value"; do not re-run the 26-task familiar-corpus design.

**Accounting traps burned into the harness (each has regression tests):** (1) turn-based transcript accounting double-counts — mid-turn rate_limit_events AND interleaved parallel-tool user events split one billed call; aggregate by message.id. (2) The CLI result envelope's top-level usage is retry-EXCLUSIVE — it silently drops billed retried calls (18/448 opus runs, asymmetric across arms because retry exposure correlates with arm behavior); T must source from modelUsage, which reconstructs total_cost_usd exactly. (3) A ~64 KiB (2^16) capture truncation voids runs and 9 opus streams are missing ~one billed call each (open: task-tracker item; consistency panel now splits billing-reconstruction from stream-completeness to watch it). (4) Keyword-graders are style-sensitive across models (opus wouldn't say "sorted"; sonnet always did) — grade on content, never a magic token.

**Product fixes (ALL LANDED 2026-07-12):** `--section A,B` comma alias (was 26% of ccx-cli errors); outline --section windowing + grep -A/-B/-C + --lines alias (+ exec-lane parity, unknown-kwarg rejection); MCP descriptions slimmed −20% (lazy loading needed NO work — Claude Code defers all MCP schemas by default; the bench only measured the tax because it pins ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH=false); terse-by-default symbol/outline (−92 to −99%/call, accuracy gate PASSED: ccx-cli 100%, mcp 96% on classic corpus). Refuted — guards do NOT block `ccx … | grep` (producer decides).

**Stage-1 flood probe (2026-07-12, ~$30): negative confirmed twice.** Flood-shaped tasks induce 5× sessions and large-seed import closures DO make baselines flood — but sonnet iterates bounded greps instead of flooding (B1 17-40%), and on the best shape ccx-cli lost H, T (2.2×), tool-result AND accuracy (76.9% vs 93.3%): the compact lane misses members on exhaustive-enumeration questions; baseline floods buy correctness. ccx's accuracy edge is task-shape-dependent: real on locate/trace/diff, reversed on exhaustive enumeration. Session-level token-savings claims are dead on both corpora; honest product story = precision tool on targeted questions + guard tail safety net + weak-model/flood-tail bounding. Two boundary-artifact lessons for any future gold: state every set-boundary clause in the prompt (test/ scope, module-scope, public-only, root), and predicates must match the prompt's LITERAL rule (C-extension modules count if an import references them).

**Haiku weak-model campaign (2026-07-13, 310 runs, $23.79):** the only non-loss token result — H +5.1% [−1.3,+12.4] (point-positive, CI grazes zero), T unresolved; accuracy edge GROWS to +5.3pts (86.6 vs 81.3), the largest measured; haiku floods 30% (2× sonnet) and ignores the ladder more (20/310 exclusions). Final deployable claim: weak models = token-neutral + biggest accuracy win; strong models = token cost for a smaller edge. NOT the savings-positive that would reopen Design C. Also that day: spawnllm 0.6.0 released (file-backed claude stdout capture kills the 64KiB Node pipe-flush truncation — filed upstream as anthropics/claude-code#77112; Response.discarded_attempts surfaces retried-away spend, folded into bench ceiling accounting only, never T); docs repositioned on the measured record (README/SKILL/cc-skills ccx fragment — codex claims-review enforced the mcp-gate 96.0<96.2 nuance and enumeration-reversal disclosure).

Sessions: 20260711T202402Z (sonnet final), 20260712T033604Z (opus final), 20260713T083836Z (haiku). Related: [[ccx-bench-sonnet-opus-result]], [[bench-spawnllm-isolation]], [[bench-fixture-guard-threshold]], [[cc-transcript-usage-accounting]].
